description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline with stages tarball, compile, snapshot, and release, using the global variables CONFIGURE_FULL, CONFIGURE_MINI and REGISTRY_HUB. In the compile stage define jobs alpine-3.18-musl, debian-11-mini, debian-12-full, fedora-39-clang and ubuntu-23.10, each using the image $REGISTRY_HUB:<distro>-... and running the shared script: mkdir build; cd build; tar xzf ../sane-backends-*.tar.gz --strip-components=1; (set -x; ./configure $CONFIGURE_OPTS); eval (set -x; make -w -O -j2 -k $MAKE_FLAGS) with job‑specific CONFIGURE_OPTS and MAKE_FLAGS variables, and debian-12-full also has an after_script that builds HTML docs with make -C build/doc html-pages, runs doxygen and stores the results as artifacts (sane-backends-*.tar.gz, lists, doc/sanei-html) that expire in 1 day. Add a make-dist job in the tarball stage using the debian-bullseye-mini image that runs git ls-files | xargs ./tools/style-check.sh, ./autogen.sh, ./tools/create-changelog.sh, ./configure and make dist, and publishes the generated tarball as an artifact for 1 day. Add a make-distcheck job in the snapshot stage that depends on debian-12-full, uses the debian-bullseye-full image, extracts the tarball, runs ./configure and make distcheck, and keeps the tarball, lists and doc artifacts for 90 days. Finally, add an upload job in the release stage that runs only on tags, is manual, needs make-dist, uses an alpine image with a before_script installing curl git jq, and executes ./tools/create-release.sh with GIT_DEPTH=3.
